dataset_name: WPA Protocol Symbols Lab — Pilot Batch (5 records)
approved_by: Pending Director approval — Assoc. Prof. Dr. Sande Smiljanov
doctrine: Преговарањето е опционално. Протоколот е апсолутен.

flag.description_en: A red field with a stylized yellow sun in the centre, with eight broadening rays extending from the central disc to the edges of the field.
flag.design_law: Закон за знамето на Република Македонија (Службен весник на РМ, бр. 47/1995); Constitution of the Republic of North Macedonia, Article 5.

anthem.design_law: Закон за химната на Република Македонија (1992).

coat_of_arms.description_en: Two curved garlands of sheaves of wheat, tobacco leaves and opium poppy fruits, tied by a ribbon decorated with traditional Macedonian folk embroidery. Within the ovoid frame: a mountain, a lake and a rising sun. Red star removed in 2009.
coat_of_arms.design_law: Constitution of the Republic of North Macedonia, Article 5; amended 2009 to remove socialist star.

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори се согласуваат на дизајнот, авторот и пропорциите на знамето. Споредна контрадикција: повеќето извори (FOTW, worldflag.org, CIA-State.gov, Wikipedia/EN) посочуваат датум на усвојување 5 октомври 1995. Еден извор (Grokipedia, упатувајќи се на Сл. весник 47/95) посочува 5 септември 1995 како датум на објавување. WPA го прифаќа 5 октомври 1995 како канонски датум на усвојување (consistent across majority of sources), но го документира контрадикторниот извор. Препорачана дипломатска корекција од МНР на РСМ за финална потврда.

sources.name: Викиизвор — Закон за знамето на Република Македонија (тековен текст)
US Department of State (citing CIA World Factbook) — Macedonia Flag
National Bank of the Republic of North Macedonia (official institutional reference); Constitution of the Republic of North Macedonia
Flags of the World (FOTW) — North Macedonia

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (pilot)

records.disclaimer: Овој запис е предмет на активен дипломатски дијалог: WPA подготвува формално барање за потврда од Министерството за надворешни работи на Република Северна Македонија за прецизна разлика помеѓу датумот на усвојување (5 октомври 1995) и датумот на објавување во „Службен весник на Република Македонија" бр. 47/1995. Канонски датум за WPA е 5 октомври 1995 до конечна државна потврда.

flag.description_en: Thirteen equal horizontal stripes of red (top and bottom) alternating with white; a blue rectangle in the upper hoist-side corner bears 50 small white five-pointed stars arranged in nine offset horizontal rows of six stars (top and bottom) alternating with rows of five stars. The 50 stars represent the 50 states; the 13 stripes represent the 13 original colonies.
flag.design_law: Flag Act of 1818 (4 U.S.C. § 1); Executive Order 10834 of 21 August 1959, fixing the 50-star design effective 4 July 1960.

anthem.composer: John Stafford Smith (melody, originally 'To Anacreon in Heaven')
anthem.design_law: Act of Congress, 3 March 1931 (46 Stat. 1508), codified at 36 U.S.C. § 301.

coat_of_arms.description_en: Great Seal of the United States. Obverse: a bald eagle with a shield of 13 paleways argent and gules, a chief azure; in its dexter talon an olive branch, in its sinister thirteen arrows; in its beak a scroll inscribed 'E PLURIBUS UNUM'. Above the eagle's head: a glory breaking through a cloud surrounding 13 stars.
coat_of_arms.design_law: Resolution of the Continental Congress, 20 June 1782; codified at 4 U.S.C. § 41.

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори (CIA World Factbook, U.S. State Department, 4 U.S.C. кодификација) се целосно согласни на сите јадрени полиња.

sources.name: 4 U.S.C. § 1 et seq. (U.S. Flag Code); 36 U.S.C. § 301 (National Anthem)
U.S. Department of State — Great Seal of the United States

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (pilot)

flag.description_en: Red field with a large yellow five-pointed star and four smaller yellow five-pointed stars (arranged in a vertical arc toward the centre of the flag) in the upper hoist-side corner. Red represents the revolution; the stars symbolise the four social classes united under the Communist Party of China, with the larger star representing the leadership of the CCP.
flag.design_law: Resolution of the First Plenary Session of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference, 27 September 1949; Law of the People's Republic of China on the National Flag, adopted 1990, amended 2009 and 2020; Constitution of the PRC (1982, amended 2018), Article 141 (Chapter IV).

anthem.design_law: Adopted provisionally 27 September 1949 by the CPPCC; formalised in the Constitution of the PRC 1982 (Article 141); Law of the PRC on the National Anthem, 1 October 2017 (effective).

coat_of_arms.description_en: National emblem of the PRC: Tiananmen Gate beneath five stars (one large, four small in arc) in gold, framed below by a gear and encircled by ears of grain — all in gold on a red disc.
coat_of_arms.designer: Lin Huiyin and design team, Tsinghua University.
coat_of_arms.design_law: Adopted by the Central People's Government Council, 20 September 1950; Constitution of the PRC, Article 142.

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори се целосно согласни. Забелешка: CIA Factbook 2023 archive го документира националниот симбол; PRC Constitution (Chapter IV) ги дава уставните рамки; NPC дава полна правна структура.

sources.name: Constitution of the People's Republic of China (1982, amended 2018), Chapter IV; Law of the PRC on the National Flag (1990, am. 2020).
Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the People's Republic of China — National Symbols

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (pilot)

capital.notes: Bonn served as the federal seat of government 1949–1999 before the federal institutions moved to Berlin; Berlin is the constitutional capital under Article 22(1) of the Basic Law.

flag.description_en: Three equal horizontal bands of black (top), red (middle), and gold (bottom). Proportion 3:5. The colours trace back to the uniforms of German soldiers during the Napoleonic Wars and were established by the Weimar Republic; readopted by the Federal Republic in 1949.
flag.design_law: Grundgesetz für die Bundesrepublik Deutschland (Basic Law), Article 22(2): 'The federal flag shall be black, red and gold.' (Promulgated 23 May 1949; design first established 9 May 1949 by the Parliamentary Council.)
flag.designer: Tradition (Weimar Republic, 1919, after the colours of the Lützow Free Corps, 1813).

anthem.title_local: Deutschlandlied (Das Lied der Deutschen) — dritte Strophe
anthem.title_en: Song of Germany — third stanza only
anthem.design_law: Exchange of letters between Federal President Richard von Weizsäcker and Federal Chancellor Helmut Kohl, 19 August 1991, confirming the third stanza as the national anthem of the reunified Germany. (Original West German adoption: exchange of letters Theodor Heuss / Konrad Adenauer, 2 May 1952.)

coat_of_arms.description_en: Bundesadler (Federal Eagle): a single-headed black eagle with open wings, red beak and red feet, on a blazoned golden-yellow shield. Based on the eagle of the Weimar Republic (1928), itself derived from the medieval imperial eagle.
coat_of_arms.designer: Tobias Schwab (Weimar version, 1928); confirmed 20 January 1950 by Federal President Theodor Heuss.
coat_of_arms.design_law: Bekanntmachung des Bundespräsidenten, 20 January 1950 (Federal Law Gazette I, p. 26).

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори се согласуваат на дизајнот, бојата и хералдичкиот опис. Споредна контрадикција околу датум на усвојување на знамето: некои извори (FOTW) посочуваат 9 мај 1949 (датум на одредба од Parlamentarischer Rat), додека WorldAtlas и официјалниот BMI извор го посочуваат 23 мај 1949 (датумот на објавување на Grundgesetz). WPA го прифаќа 23 мај 1949 како канонски датум (датум на ефективност на Уставот), со 9 мај 1949 документиран како adoption_date_first.

sources.name: Bundesministerium des Innern und für Heimat (BMI) — Germany's State Symbols (official)
Grundgesetz für die Bundesrepublik Deutschland, Article 22 (Federal Law Gazette)

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (pilot)

records.disclaimer: This record is subject to active diplomatic dialogue: WPA is preparing a formal request to the Bundesministerium des Innern und für Heimat (BMI) for confirmation of the canonical adoption date of the Federal flag. WPA currently treats 23 May 1949 (promulgation of the Grundgesetz) as canonical; 9 May 1949 (vote of the Parliamentary Council) is preserved as adoption_date_first.

capital.notes: Capital since 21 April 1960 (previously Rio de Janeiro).

flag.description_en: A green field with a large yellow rhombus in the centre, bearing a blue disc which forms a celestial globe depicting 27 small white five-pointed stars spanned by a white equatorial curved band with the national motto 'Ordem e Progresso' (Order and Progress) written in green. The 27 stars represent the 26 federal states and the Federal District; their positions reproduce the southern sky over Rio de Janeiro on the morning of the Proclamation of the Republic, 15 November 1889.
flag.design_law: Decreto nº 4, 19 November 1889 (original design); Lei nº 8.421, signed 11 May 1992 by President Fernando Collor de Mello, published in Diário Oficial da União 12 May 1992 (current 27-star configuration). Per WPA editorial decision (Sande, 23 May 2026), the canonical adoption date is the date of official publication (12 May 1992); the signing date (11 May 1992) is retained in this design_law note as historical reference.
flag.designer: Raimundo Teixeira Mendes (with Manuel Pereira Reis, Miguel Lemos, Décio Villares).

anthem.design_law: Decreto nº 171, 20 January 1890 (melody made official); Decreto nº 15.671, 6 September 1922 (current lyrics officially adopted by Duque-Estrada); Lei nº 5.700, 1 September 1971 (consolidated law on national symbols).

coat_of_arms.description_en: A circular blue shield bearing the Southern Cross constellation on a white star-studded field, surrounded by a green border with 27 silver stars (representing the 26 states and the Federal District). The shield rests on a five-pointed yellow star with rays. Two branches frame the composition: coffee on the dexter side and tobacco on the sinister side. Below: a blue ribbon inscribed 'REPÚBLICA FEDERATIVA DO BRASIL / 15 DE NOVEMBRO DE 1889'.
coat_of_arms.designer: Artur Zauer (original design); updated by Lei 8.421/1992.
coat_of_arms.design_law: Lei nº 5.700, 1 September 1971; Lei nº 8.421, signed 11 May 1992, published in Diário Oficial da União 12 May 1992 (current configuration).

records.verification_status_notes: Сите три извори се согласуваат на дизајнот и бројот на ѕвезди (27). Споредна разлика околу датумот на тековното знаме: повеќето извори (CIA Factbook, Wikipedia, текст на Lei 8.421) посочуваат 11 мај 1992 (датум на потпишување на законот од Претседателот Collor). Еден извор (worldflag.org) посочува 12 мај 1992 (датум на објавување во Diário Oficial da União). Per WPA Director editorial decision (Sande, 23 May 2026), WPA го прифаќа 12 мај 1992 (датум на објавување) како канонски датум на адопција, со фуснота за потписот од 11 мај 1992.

sources.name: Lei nº 8.421, 11 May 1992 — Presidência da República, Casa Civil (Planalto)
Lei nº 5.700 de 1 de setembro de 1971 — Símbolos Nacionais (Planalto)

audit_trail.created_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer
audit_trail.verified_by: Claude WPA Academic & Lab Excellence Officer (pilot)

records.disclaimer: WPA editorial decision (Director, 23 May 2026): The canonical adoption date for the current 27-star flag is recorded as 12 May 1992 (Diário Oficial da União publication of Lei nº 8.421). The signing date of 11 May 1992 by President Fernando Collor de Mello is documented as a historical footnote in the design_law field.

changelog: v1.1.0 (2026-05-23): Director-approved revisions per Sande 23 May 2026: (1) Brazil flag adoption_date_current changed from 1992-05-11 to 1992-05-12 (Diário Oficial publication date per WPA editorial decision); 11 May 1992 retained as signing date in note. (2) Macedonia: disclaimer added announcing DCP outreach to MFA. (3) Germany: disclaimer added announcing DCP outreach to BMI. (4) Brazil: disclaimer added documenting the editorial choice of publication-date as canonical.
